curation_notes: Re-curated with updated guidelines. Patch commit 9332d42 identified from v4.1.2 tag and PR #34. Only one non-test source file changed (lib/index.js). The vulnerability is in the randomDigits() function which used Math.floor(buffer[i] / 25.6) to map random bytes to digits 0-9. Since 256 is not evenly divisible by 10, digits 0-5 each had a 26/256 probability while digits 6-9 had 25/256, creating insufficient entropy (CWE-331). The fix uses rejection sampling, discarding byte values >= 250 so remaining values map evenly via modulo 10. No sibling files exist — lib/ contains only index.js. Vulnerability class crypto-weakness confirmed from CWE-331 and the nature of the fix. L3 hints differentiated from L1/L2 by specifying the digit generation subsystem and the division-based mapping mechanism.
